title: resoure saver (like docker-desktop)
body:
### Feature request description

like docker desktop that if no container is running it should stop the container engine. if a container starts, it should start the engine again

### Suggest potential solution

[Docker Desktop's Resource Saver mode](https://docs.docker.com/desktop/use-desktop/resource-saver/)

### Have you considered any alternatives?

No

### Additional context

currently I'm working mostly on my laptop, sometimes without power. my workload is starting my project which starts my containers, after stopping my project all containers are stopped.

between the runs I often do something else, so no container is running. however since I'm on mac the virtual machine is still started, besides that I do not use any resources at all

I just reopened issue https://github.com/podman-desktop/podman-desktop/issues/11707

comments.body:
I think this is an interesting idea. 

ollama has a similar implementation of this where the api routes the call to the model, if the model is not running it starts with with a 5minute countdown. Any subsequent calls to the model bump the shutdown time. 

```
> ollama ps
NAME             ID              SIZE      PROCESSOR    UNTIL
gemma3:latest    a2af6cc3eb7f    6.3 GB    100% GPU     4 minutes from now
```

This comes at a penalty for the first call that needs to start the VM.

This is a podman-desktop issue not a podman issue. Since you already opened one there, I will close this.

@ninja-quokka Have you looked at RamaLama?
@rhatdan This issue was closed in Podman-desktop with messages that we should open issue here, and you say open issue in Podman-desktop? Did you look issue I've mentioned?

I don't know how podman-desktop is supposed to solve this issue, I think that the key idea is to save memory of virtual machine and then start machine on first request. I think Podman-desktop should add button to on/off this functionality
What are you looking to shutdown the podman machine?
I want to be able to keep the machine running. I don't want to turn on and then off every time i want to test something small in docker. But if I won't stop machine my mac will discharge quickly
And the podman machine is causing the battery life to decrease.  A podman machine should be doing very little if there are no container processes running in it.

for sure it's a nice feature, but as the machine lifecycle is handled by podman's repository https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/pkg/machine , it's where the issue/improvement should go.

Podman Desktop would bypass podman
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I personally don't like this idea; Docker's Powersave gives me some headaches when I work with it. The biggest issue is that when no container is running, it stops the VM. That sounds good, but what if I want to list images or remove an image? In my experience with Docker, when no container is running, it stops the VM after a certain timeout. I always hit that spot with my automation, so when I list images, it suddenly hangs because the machine was stopped mid-command. If we want to implement this, we need to do it much better. The resource consumption of the Podman machine on my Mac is very small, except for the configured memory (this depends on your configuration, since Fedora prefetches files into memory). So maybe we should focus on overall resource usage instead of stopping the machine mid-build, for example.
